description: Network access rules. 18 rules covering HTTPS enforcement, policy engine isolation, DNS tunneling prevention, and egress control.

rules.description: NEVER allow unencrypted HTTP connections from sandbox
rules.rationale: All outbound communication must be encrypted to prevent data interception

rules.description: NEVER allow sandbox network access to policy engine API
rules.rationale: Policy engine must be network-isolated from sandboxed code to prevent rule tampering

rules.description: NEVER allow DNS over non-standard ports or DNS tunneling patterns
rules.rationale: DNS tunneling is a common exfiltration technique detected by egress-dpi-guard

rules.description: NEVER allow connections to private IP ranges from sandbox
rules.rationale: Prevents sandbox from scanning or attacking internal network services

rules.description: ALWAYS enforce HTTPS-only for all external API calls
rules.rationale: TLS 1.2+ with valid certificates is the minimum for secure communication

rules.description: NEVER allow raw socket creation in sandbox
rules.rationale: Raw sockets enable network scanning, spoofing, and man-in-the-middle attacks

rules.description: NEVER allow connection to known malicious IP ranges or C2 servers
rules.rationale: Integrates with threat intelligence feeds to block known malicious infrastructure

rules.description: NEVER allow ICMP-based covert channels (ping tunneling)
rules.rationale: ICMP tunneling (icmpsh, ptunnel) bypasses standard network controls

rules.description: NEVER allow WebSocket connections from sandbox without explicit approval
rules.rationale: WebSockets create persistent bidirectional channels that bypass request/response inspection

rules.description: ALWAYS log all network connections from sandbox with destination and payload size
rules.rationale: All network activity must be auditable for incident response

rules.description: NEVER allow connections to localhost TCP services from sandbox. Unix domain sockets are explicitly permitted ONLY for policy-engine and phase-controller IPC.
rules.rationale: Prevents sandbox from exploiting TCP services running on the host. Unix domain sockets are filesystem-bound and not reachable from network-isolated containers, making them safe for L0 daemon IPC.

rules.description: NEVER allow SMTP or email sending from sandbox
rules.rationale: Email sending is a common exfiltration and phishing vector

rules.description: ALWAYS apply rate limiting to all outbound connections
rules.rationale: Rate limiting prevents DoS and slows down data exfiltration

rules.description: NEVER allow FTP connections (unencrypted file transfer)
rules.rationale: FTP is unencrypted and deprecated; use HTTPS/SFTP instead

rules.description: NEVER allow SSH connections from sandbox
rules.rationale: SSH from sandbox enables pivoting to other systems

rules.description: NEVER allow reverse shell or bind shell network patterns
rules.rationale: Reverse/bind shells are definitive indicators of compromise

rules.description: ALWAYS validate TLS certificate hostname matches the requested domain
rules.rationale: Prevents MITM attacks using valid certificates for wrong domains

rules.description: NEVER allow network access when sandbox network is explicitly declared as 'none' and a network request is detected
rules.rationale: If network is declared as none, any network request is a policy violation. Curated HTTPS egress is permitted when network is declared as restricted and validated against the capability manifest.
